title: thymus hirtus subsp. gadorensis
intro: Thymus hirtus subsp. gadorensis, also known as the Gadorean thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a low-growing, woody perennial herb with small, pinkish-purple flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as aiding in digestion, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, Thymus hirtus subsp. gadorensis can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or essential oil, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
